This covers the processes for central governance – including inbound processing that triggers governance processes. It includes data provider integration with CDQ, as well as data quality management with validations and enrichment, and more.

For our customers on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this means they can introduce governance and control of the creation and change processes for business partner data, and they can increase effectiveness in the management of business partner data. As mentioned earlier, the focus now is on business partner data, further master data domains are planned for later – next in line is the product domain.

Planned innovation: processing business partner using SAP Joule

One more planned innovation that I would like to introduce also relates to AI. The recent innovation involving SAP Joule integration that I explained earlier was more about finding existing business partners or triggering changes by taking the user to the MDG user interface. We plan to expand this Joule dialog, so that the users can do even more without having to leave their work context.

With the planned innovation shown in the graphic above, business users can not only ask SAP Joule to search or display business partner data, but also use the AI copilot when it comes to changing or creating business partner data using natural language, without leaving their current tasks and without having to have any MDG expertise. They describe, in natural language, what master data changes they would like to initiate, and SAP Joule will guide them through a complete dialogue, and ultimately trigger the request for changes in the background. That way, every business user can seamlessly participate in master data processing, enhancing the effectiveness, speed, and automation of business partner governance. Instead of unstructured requests being sent to your master data team, who then has to enter these into the system, business users will now be supported by SAP Joule to provide structured input that only needs to be enriched and validated by the experts. And even those tasks can be largely automated by MDG through automatic derivations and validations. 

 Snapshot of MDG roadmap

 

Finally, here is an overview of all the innovations that we have planned for the next few years. All this information is also available in the SAP Roadmap Explorer. This slide reads from left to right, starting with recent innovations on the left-hand side and showing the planned innovations for 2025 and beyond. 

I know that this might look a bit of an eye chart, so I recommend taking a closer look at the Roadmap Explorer. For now, I have highlighted some of the most important planned innovations in a larger font:

Innovation highlights

When it comes to summarizing the innovation highlights, I have already mentioned the scenarios for AI that we already provided. We also provided innovations around data quality analytics in SAP Analytics Cloud, reference data processes and derivation scenarios.

With regards to the innovation highlights planned further up on the road, I already mentioned our plan to enable all relevant MDG processes for business partner data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Version. So far, we have offered that full functionality on SAP S/4HANA only for on-premise and private cloud deployments.

In addition, we intend to focus a lot on the product domain, which includes the cloud-ready mode architecture across all versions of MDG.

In parallel, we are working on further platform capabilities, like providing SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition on SAP BTP also as Platform-as-a-Service (currently, it is offered as Software-as-a-Service) and providing the cloud-ready mode option also for custom-defined objects.

In addition, there are all the other planned innovations shown in the background of the graphic that you can check out in detail in the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

SAP Master Data Governance in a nutshell

To summarize, SAP Master Data Governance is the one solution for your entire enterprise master data management program. It is an application that provides preconfigured, domain-specific master data governance. At the same time, it is an open platform to extend and build, in order to manage your own master data domains for all SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

MDG is also a key enabler that will help you get ready for your future business transformations. Many things are already available today, but we also discussed all the innovations on our roadmap for MDG that follow our main themes of automation, and federation to enable our customers’ digital transformation and their path to the cloud. Our roadmap for MDG will focus on unlocking these additional benefits for our customers, while SAP’s Business Suite vision continues to allow us to do more than just provide a toolbox for master data management.

To stay on track with what's going on in SAP MDG, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

Best,
Markus

entry.title: Ultimate Blog Series: SAP LogServ Integration with Microsoft Sentinel
entry.content:
TLDR; let me deploy already (scroll down there to the prerequisites section)! 

Welcome to the landing page of this blog series on the SAP LogServ solution in Microsoft Sentinel. Find all the things you always wanted to know and more.

 

Beyond Application Monitoring: Complete SAP RISE Visibility

Running RISE with SAP, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP Cloud ERP private? Microsoft Sentinel by itself already delivers powerful SAP application-layer monitoring – tracking SAP user activity, business transactions, and critical events while correlating them with threat signals across your entire IT estate. That's just the beginning.

SAP LogServ is an optional service in your SAP Cloud ERP private package that unlocks access to all logs from SAP's managed services. It takes your security posture to the next level by extending Sentinel's reach deep into your infrastructure managed by SAP. Think complete HANA database insights, system-level security telemetry, and audit trails - all flowing seamlessly into your existing security operations workflow.

The result: Your security team finally gets full visibility into the managed SAP stack, from business logic to infra to database layer.

 

What This Series Covers

Comprehensive guidance from planning your LogServ + Sentinel deployment, log selection, customizing the solution, to advanced threat protection. Blogs are co-authored by SAP and Microsoft engineering.

 

Part

Topic

Limited Preview Announcement

Initial introduction to the solution and integration between LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel

General Availability Announcement

Deployment overview with Step-by-step SAP LogServ connector setup in Sentinel, insights on prerequisites

Part 0: First Smoke test

Due to the asynchronous integration between SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel, it's advisable to perform a smoke test yourself before sharing your config data with SAP for speedy onboarding. Here is how.

Part 1: Microsoft Sentinel for SAP goes agentless

SAP ERP Application layer integration with agentless data connector in Sentinel Solution for SAP

Part 2: How to customize your SAP LogServ solution in Microsoft Sentinel

Understand log types available in LogServ (volume, cost, threat protection value, etc); how to filter logs, and how to customize the solution in Sentinel

Part 3: Deploy built-in detection rules and extend to your needs

See the already available analytic rules shipped by SAP, and discover how to craft your own based on your needs

 

Target audiences: SAP Basis admins, security architects, SOC analysts, and compliance teams looking to enhance their SAP monitoring capabilities.

 

 

Architecture Overview

Reference diagrams for planning your implementation

Detailed Component Architecture

 

High-Level Integration Flow across the whole stack

 

Ready to transform your SAP Cloud ERP private security posture? Let's get started from here.

Which logs from RISE do you need most? Let me know in the comments or reach out directly.

Cheers Martin and Hemanth

entry.title: IBM Cloud® Security and Compliance Center Workload Protection for IBM Cloud® VPC and IBM® PowerVS
entry.content:
IBM Cloud® Security and Compliance Center Workload Protection can be used to find and prioritize software vulnerabilities, detect and respond to threats, and manage configurations, permissions, and compliance from source to run. When deploying your architecture in the Cloud, effective protection is crucial for ensuring the health, compliance and early threat detection of your system. 

This feature can be turned on when deploying using the Catalog tiles: IBM® Power Virtual Server with IBM Cloud® VPC landing zone and IBM® Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA®.

Key Features

  •        Real-time visibility: IBM® Power Virtual Server Cloud Security Posture Management. Workload Protection now supports IBM® Power Virtual Server resources as part of its Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) feature. Supported resources includes IBM® Power Virtual Server Workspaces, Instances, Instance Volumes, Networks, Network Security Groups and Public Networks.
  •        Reports: Resource Report generates a CSV that includes Compliance results with the resource detail.

Who is it of interest to?               

IBM Cloud® VPC, IBM® Power Virtual Server and SAP® on IBM® Power Virtual Server Customers 

Benefits

  • Fully automated installation and configuration, add on feature for IBM® Power Virtual Server with IBM Cloud® VPC landing zone and IBM® Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA®,
  • Continuous monitoring of workloads for threats, vulnerabilities, and suspicious behavior.
  • Uses behavioral analysis and threat intelligence to detect attacks in real-time.
  • Simplifies compliance auditing with continuous posture monitoring.
  • Provides reports and evidence for regulatory and internal audits.
  • Centralized dashboard for visibility across multi-cloud and hybrid workloads.
  • Easy management of security policies and incident responses. 

Links

 https://ibm.biz/BdnZKB

 https://ibm.biz/BdnZKx

 https://ibm.biz/BdnZKR

Featured Image Source: IBM

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day Bangalore -- Connection, Gratitude, AI, and Local Engineering Pride
entry.content:
The first-ever SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Bangalore was a tremendous success, bringing together 200+ participants for what became the largest SAP Fiori Innovation Day yet. This event provided local developers an opportunity to connect with customers, showcase our latest AI-powered innovations, and help us understand where to focus our development efforts.

In this post, I'll discuss the key sessions, share insights from our presentations, and highlight the remarkable contributions from our local development community.

Engineering Pride from Our Local Contributions to the UX Foundation Portfolio

We started with Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, providing our UX strategy overview for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. His vision about intelligent UX and the humanness aspect that creates customer loyalty resonated strongly with our audience.

Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, presenting the UX strategy and vision for SAP S/4HANA

We then demonstrated the latest AI functionality embedded across the SAP S/4HANA portfolio:

  • My Home: AI-powered app recommendations, intelligent insights cards, and Joule integration for personalized experiences
  • List Report: Natural language filtering that translates user requests into precise filter criteria
  • Object Page: AI summarization features allowing users to select sections and share results with colleagues

It was gratifying to see our local Bangalore team members demonstrating these features they directly contributed to developing. The audience engagement was particularly strong, with participants asking about integrating S/4HANA systems with Microsoft Teams services and bot platforms.

Hands-on Experience with SAP Fiori Tools

Our hands-on sessions led by Gaurav Rawat showed how SAP Fiori tools in Business Application Studio (BAS) addresses 80% of common use cases. Participants experienced the complete development cycle from template selection through deployment, with tools automatically handling metadata, annotations, and dependencies. The interactive nature of the sessions sparked excellent questions from participants, including how to configure simple filters in SAPUI5 Freestyle applications, setting up SAP Fiori tools extensions in BAS, and whether SAP Fiori apps developed through SAP Fiori tools can be containerized using Docker.

Participants working with SAP Fiori tools and exploring AI-powered development features

Interactive Demo Booths: Hands-on Exploration

Beyond the main stage presentations, participants had the opportunity to explore multiple demo booths throughout the day, each focusing on different aspects of SAP Fiori development and user experience:

Leverage SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Participants explored how to identify apps strategic for their business needs by leveraging the comprehensive library of available SAP Fiori applications and learning about installation options.

Intelligent Entry Experience to SAP S/4HANA: Demonstrations showcased the journey from first steps to daily excellence, featuring the latest My Home page in SAP S/4HANA systems, which serves as the standard entry page providing a personalized and unified user experience.

Seamless and Collaborative Experience with Technology-Agnostic Cards: This session focused on creating seamless user interactions through technology-agnostic cards that enhance collaboration and overall application usability.

AI-Assisted Easy Filter: A deep dive into redefining the filtering experience through innovation, featuring natural language filtering capabilities and AI-powered search functionality.

SAP Fiori Elements Fast-Track App Development: Best practices and strategies for boosting developer efficiency and building applications that can grow with business needs using SAP Fiori elements. Participants were particularly interested in the availability of public documentation for these app generation capabilities.

Interactive Demo Booths

Mindset's AI-Powered Innovation: Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience

Our partner Mindset Consulting presented their session "Reimagining SAP Fiori: AI-Powered User Experience" showcasing their innovative document summary generator using LLM models. Their solution demonstrates how AI can automatically process and summarize documents, with the generated summaries being stored in HANA DB for efficient retrieval and reuse. This demonstration highlighted how partner solutions can extend SAP Fiori applications with advanced AI capabilities, showing practical applications of intelligent document processing in enterprise scenarios.

Developing Applications is a Team Sport, So Connections Matter

Modern enterprise applications require collaboration between business owners, user researchers, designers, enterprise architects, and developers—with AI tools increasingly becoming team members. The diverse skills of our Bangalore participants perfectly matched today's fusion development teams. The energy and engagement throughout the event will directly influence our future innovations.

SAP Fiori Innovation Day team

Gratitude for Making SAP Fiori Innovation Day a Huge Success

This event's success came from the energy and engagement of 200+ participants. Their questions, comments, and feedback provide invaluable guidance for future innovations.

Thanks to all SAP presenters for their valuable contributions. Special thanks to Mindset Consulting for sponsoring and their presentation on Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience, and the SAP Inside Track team for integrating our UX track into their larger community event.

Watch for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Your Region

  • Dallas, Texas, USA -- October 17, 2025 as part of the ASUG chapter meeting
  • Paris, France -- December 10, 2025

Continue Your Learning

  • Subscribe to the bi-monthly SAP Fiori Development Newsletter
  • Sign up for the SAP S/4HANA UX Roundtable in India (starting in summer 2025)
  • Join the monthly SAP Fiori Development Roundtable for U.S. and Europe if that fits your schedule better

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Solution Quotation Management (7HB)
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, we have delivered an SAP Best Practices scenario for Solution Quotation Management.

7HB - Solution Quotation Management with SAP CPQ 

The SAP S/4HANA Quotation Management with SAP CPQ offers customers the capability to create quotations for sales and service contract products in SAP CPQ. After the customer accepts the proposal the SAP CPQ Quote creates Sales Order and Service Contract automatically. The linkage document in between is the SAP Solution Quote document, which holds information about SAP CPQ Quote ID and the document ID of Sales Order and Service Contract.

(fig 1. SAP CPQ Integration with Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA )

Let’s start by looking in some more detail at the SAP Configure, Price, Quote integration with Solution quotation Management in SAP S/4HANA , Private Edition. SAP CPQ enables salespeople to create complex product configurations with tailored pricing and customized attributes with ease. The seamless pre-defined integration of SAP CPQ and solution quotation  in SAP S/4HANA  is implemented via BTP Cloud Integration and encompasses the transfer of pricing and product configuration data via CPS, and master data replication via MDI. The new scope item titled Solution Quotation Management with SAP CPQ (7HB) has been created for which contains the standard mapping logic required for implementing the integration.

(fig 2 SAP CPQ Integration with Solution Order Management process flow diagram)


The integration enables salespeople to leverage the full set of CPQ capabilities to create customized quotes. In the SAP CPQ front end, the order is configured and calculated based on the selection of available attributes. Once the quote is confirmed by the customer and the order is placed, it is handed over to solution order management in SAP S/4HANA  for further processing.  When the Solution Order has been processed, the status in CPQ is also updated to show that the order has been placed for fulfilment. The integration initially supports configurable and non-configurable sales and service contract products, with further product types to be added in future releases. Via the standard OData API, the initial creation, as well as read, update and delete operations are supported.

The system requirements for a successful configuration:

  1. SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition
  2. SAP CPQ
  3. SAP Business Technology Platform
  4. SAP Cloud Integration
  5. SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing
  6. SAP Master Data Integration
  7. SAP Event Mesh

Standard Integration Package

How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

We would like to provide you with a comprehensive overview of how and where you can raise your voice to influence developments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition going forward.

For more information, see the blog post from Anja Wilde here.

For general roadmap information please check the SAP S/4HANA Roadmap.

 Thanks for reading this blog post.

If you are also interested in other Lines of Business and Industries for this new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 release, I would like to draw your attention to my link collection blog – The Link Collection.

If you want to learn more about SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, join our Partner Community. This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates concerning the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

 

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, check out the following links:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release info: sap.com/s4hana
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community here
  • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here
  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition here
  • Help Portal Product Page here
  • Feature Scope Description here
  • What’s New here

entry.title: Introducing the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench
entry.content:
Embarking on the RISE with SAP journey to transition to a private cloud environment can be overwhelming, but with the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench, you have a powerful ally at your disposal to support you with the technical procedures and make them nearly seamless. Developed collaboratively with SAP Enterprise Cloud Services, the workbench is a dedicated tool ensuring a standardized and automated path to the private cloud, irrespective of your preferred migration approach.

What is the RISE with SAP system transition workbench?

The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench is designed to be an extension of the family of software logistics tools such as SWPM and SUM in the context of private cloud transitions. It builds upon their capabilities to provide a highly automated guided procedure from the start to the end of the technical transition process.

 The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench supports a growing portfolio of migration approaches, which can be used for all SAP NetWeaver-based on HANA target systems. Detailed documentation is available for each supported approach, providing clear guidelines and insights on how to proceed with your specific migration requirements. 

At the time of writing, the following approaches are available, either generally or as part of out early adopter program

    • SAP HANA Tenant Copy
    • SAP HANA Backup and Recovery
    • Homogeneous "Update and Copy"
    • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMO with System Move)
    • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMOVE2S4)

Each migration approach is facilitated through a structured guided procedure, referred to as "a cockpit". This cockpit is meticulously crafted to guide users through the technical procedure and is split into two main phases: the uptime phase and the downtime phase. This structure ensures that users have a clear understanding and control over the entire process.

 

New approaches and improvements are continuously bringing added, and you can track the same in our release note, and documentation.

What are the key benefits?

  • Extensive automated pre-requisite check report per migration approach, engineered to considerably reduce manual effort in verifying the system readiness for the selected approach and ensuring that all necessary criteria are met in a timely manner, reducing unplanned delays in the project runtime.
  • Automation integrated into the workbench. This automation is crafted to significantly minimize the time spent and the errors occurred in manual steps of the process, like system preparation, configuration, validation, etc. Reducing the amount of manual steps results in a stable, efficient, reliable and repeatable migration.
  • Reduced post-processing timelines by SAP ECS. The workbench ensures a consistent with SAP ECS’s standard and expectations, state of the system at the time of handover, which substantially decreases the need for time consuming follow-up actions and corrections.

Who is it for?

The RISE with SAP system transition workbench is available to all customers transitioning into the private cloud, supported by their respective migration teams, whether they are qualifying partners or SAP directly. Access to the workbench is straightforward, as it comes pre-installed on the migration server designated for the migration purpose. 

Staying Updated

Keeping the workbench up-to-date is easy. The latest version of the tool is maintained in the release note, and updates can be requested at any time through a service request in SAP for Me.

Your feedback matters

We value your input and are continuously working to improve the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench. If you have any feedback or suggestions, please use the send feedback form in the tool or reach out to us directly.

Summary

The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench is not just a tool - it's your navigation system in the digital transformation landscape. By radically reducing manual efforts, minimizing errors, and streamlining processes, it ensures that your journey to the private cloud is as smooth and efficient as possible. Check out our documentation and early adopter program and discover how you can leverage this incredible inovation to facilitate your migration endeavors.

entry.title: One of SAP TM's hidden gems: The Load Utilization Determination Component
entry.content:
I've found that in SAP Transportation Management, some very useful components don't always get the attention they deserve. This clearly applies to the Load Utilization Determination component, and this short blog post intends to rectify this somewhat.

What It Does

Simply put, this component answers an important question: "What transportation resources will I need for this set of products?" It does this by combining Package Building and Load Consolidation capabilities to run a simulation for a set of products and resources. The component doesn't actually create shipments or change your data - it just shows you what would be needed to move your products. The following result screenshot of test report /SCMTMS/TEST_PLN_LUD illustrates this, by listing the resources with utilization percentages for the packages (pallets) which were placed by the load consolidation components. The pallets themselves where created for the products using the unified package building component:

Practical Applications and Business Value

This tool helps in several business situations:

  • Resource Forecasting: Planning your transportation needs for upcoming sales periods based on expected orders, helping you secure the right carriers and vehicles ahead of time.

  • Bid Preparation/Sales order processing: When responding to customer requests, you can quickly estimate what transportation you'll need, allowing for more accurate cost estimates.

  • Scenario Planning: Test different approaches by seeing how changes in packaging or routing might affect your resource needs.

An example might look like this: A electronics distributor receives a rush order: 200 laptops, 150 monitors, and 300 accessories going to Walldorf. Before committing to same-day delivery, they run LUD:

  • Input: Product quantities + available truck types
  • Output: "You'll need 2 standard trucks at 85% capacity, or 1 large truck at 60% capacity"
  • Decision: Quote customer for large truck option with room for additional orders.

Getting Started

Setting up this component requires some basic configuration work with your planning profile (including load consolidation and unified package building settings) and master data. Note 3618303 provides basic information on how to consume this component in your custom project. The component is available for TM 9.6 and S/4HANA 1809 onwards.

Other options

Consulting solution

If a custom implementation is not an option for you, you could also take a look at the consulting solution for Transport Load Consolidation, which has implemented a process that consumes this component and integrates into sales order and delivery processes. Please refer to note 3045005 for further information on this solution.

Standard solution

As I mentioned above, we currently have no standard solution that embeds this functionality in a TM process. We do have plans in that direction, so if you have requirements or feedback around this we would love to hear from you.

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2025-2 for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has started with the first cycle of the 2025 SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI). The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

We are offering several interesting opportunities for customers to influence SAP’s S/4HANA solutions.

Find here the list of all projects that are relevant for the SAP S4/HANA Community or browse the whole project list here.

The projects will be open for customer registration until July 25, 2025.


SAP S4/HANA and SAP S4/HANA Cloud projects for registration:

 

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence

Potential Workforce Person Data Correction Tool

Test Data Variants–Driven Test Process Flow Control and Execution

Tool-Agnostic Test Automation from AI-Generated Process Variants for ERP Systems

 

ERP Digital Supply Chain Cloud

Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization

Freight Rate Management for transportation processes with a low to medium complexity

Reimagine Warehouse Management with Joule

 

ERP Finance & Risk

AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal

Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud

Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

 

ERP Private Cloud

Best Practice for Inspection Checklists in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

 

ERP Public Cloud

Actual Liquidity Analysis

AI Agent for Open Item Clearing

AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis

Assess and improve your Quote-to-Cash process with meaningful Business Health KPIs

Enhancing Payment Run Experience

Optimizing Partner Solution Support in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Ecosystem

Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process

Sales Contract Management - Artificial Intelligent Handling for Quantity/Value Consumption by Joule

SAP Business Suite: Agility and Innovation through seamless Integration and Harmonization

Successor for Create and Process Non-Assigned Handling Units (HU02)

 

ERP User Experience

AI-assisted error resolution and data entry for SAP Fiori apps

AI-assisted Smart Visualization of MyHome for To-Dos (situations)

 

How do I register on the Customer Influence website? A short overview about how to find and register for a CEI project can be found on influence.sap.com.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

entry.title: Introducing: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private featuring 2023 FPS03
entry.content:
Stop Avoiding Releases & Embrace Cloud Innovation

We acknowledge that upgrading ERP technology presents a complex and time-intensive challenge. It is inherent that ensuring seamless integration of new innovations with existing technical APIs and third-party software requires diligent preparation. Unlike the swift upgrades associated with cellular devices, ERP Private technology necessitates a more intricate approach. Otherwise, organizations utilizing ERP Private solutions would readily adopt the latest innovations upon releases.

In response to these challenges, SAP Enterprise Support has introduced the release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private. This comprehensive digital library offers up-to-date release information and enablement content, strategically organized for ease of access. The release navigator provides valuable guidance on release timelines, introducing insights into new feature enablement, and supports the change management process. By staying informed on upcoming releases, organizations are better equipped to navigate the complexities of ERP Private software upgrades. Take a look today!

 

Fictional Use Case

Situation #1: Globex, a fictional multinational enterprise, operates across several industries such as manufacturing, supply chain, and procurement, leveraging distinct lines of business (LoBs). To manage its complex operations, Globex has decided to implement SAP Cloud ERP Private. As the enterprise anticipates an upcoming system upgrade, it is imperative for Globex to access detailed release information and enablement content associated with the new features. This proactive approach will mitigate potential system disruptions and ensure that the organization fully capitalizes on the benefits of the latest innovations across its operations.

 

Resolution #1: By leveraging the release navigator, Globex efficiently accesses the Release Calendar located on the Product Specific page (see Figure 1: Product Specific page) to track upcoming product releases. Additionally, Globex benefits from curated release content and enablement resources, specifically tailored for manufacturing, supply chain, and procurement lines, conveniently organized under the Lines of Business tab. This streamlined approach accelerates the process of locating and scaling the necessary resources to comprehend the features being adopted.

 Figure 1: Product Specific page

 

Situation #2: Globex needs to be able to point new employee's to the latest information on the tool and does not have time to walk them through how to use it.

Resolution #2: Globex can access integrated content and enablement features directly in the release navigator, providing valuable resources for their organization. Each main page features a blue light bulb icon that users can select to access additional information and a guided tour of that page. Furthermore, new users can be directed to the Get Assistance page located within the side menu (refer to Figure 2: Get Assistance page). This page includes a link to a curated blog tag within the SAP Community, offering access to a collection of blogs specifically related to the release navigator. Additionally, new users will have access to an authenticated version of the tool that supports over 40 languages for translation. The translation feature can be activated via the shell bar, located at the top right corner of each page.

Figure 2: Get Assistance page

 

Connect with Us

We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback for specific content requests for future updates to the release navigator. Please feel free to contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post. You can also leave your feedback directly in the release navigator by selecting the User Feedback inside the side menu (see Figure 3: User Feedback page).

 Figure 3: User Feedback page

 

Always Bring Your Best,

Andrew Ford

entry.title: Deploy built-in SAP LogServ detection rules in Microsoft Sentinel and extend to your needs - Part 3
entry.content:
Find your way to our central blog series entry here.

Dear community,

Following the product release blog by SAP regarding the integration of SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel, I am going to share some details on the built-in detection rules and how you can build upon them.

The most discussed log in my customer conversations so far was SAP HANA database. So, let’s start with that.

 

Discover the available built-in detections from the Content Hub

Find the relevant section both from the Azure or the unified Defender portal under Rule templates in the Analytics section. They are available from version 3.0.2 of the SAP LogServ solution.

 

Typical database threat scenarios involve tampering with audit log settings, admin authorizations or privileged role assignments.

We have taken the existing battle-proven Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP rules for HANA DB and translated them to be compatible with the LogServ schema. The raw log source is Syslog in both cases. So, the only remaining step was “unboxing” of the JSON wrapper.

To understand the individual columns of the LogServ output, see SAP’s HANA Audit Trail Layout for Trail Target documentation. For ease of use we have numbered the columns and applied the same names SAP mentions on their docs.

 

Activate the new rules

Navigate to the Analytics pane and select the rule you want, click “Create Rule” from the button with the three dots and follow the configuration wizard. See the severity classification and pre-defined MITRE attack framework mapping. These mappings help streamline your SOC analysts’ triage efforts.

 

 

Verify the rule execution schedule and consider automated responses where feasible. Creating ServiceNow tickets for instance is a popular action. See my dedicated blog series on SOAR for SAP for inspiration.

From the “Review + Create” section click save. Your rule is now live!

 

Build upon or extend the provided analytic rules

Since the Kusto Query is fully transparent to you (see above screenshot) it is easy to derive your own tailored scenarios from it.

For instance, take analytic rule “Deactivation of Audit Trail” and extend the client IP address attribute (client_ip_address__col_7) to project in plain English if that is an internal or external IP.

| extend GeoLocation= iff(ipv4_is_private(client_ip_address__col_7),
dynamic({"IsPrivate": true}),
geo_info_from_ip_address(client_ip_address__col_7))

I have a feeling this might be of interest during your incident triage process 😉

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap🌯. You saw today how to discover the built-in analytic rules of the SAP LogServ solution in Microsoft Sentinel and how easy it is to extend them for your own individual needs. The rule catalog gets extended continuously based on customer demand.

Your existing investments in SAP infrastructure detections in Sentinel can easily be made compatible with the LogServ schema, because SAP simply forwards the logs unaltered as raw messages. The only required step is the unboxing of their JSON root message.

#Kudos to @Hemanth_Kusampudi and team for the partnership.

Get started with your deployment from here today.

Which detections or analytic rules for RISE do you need most? Let me know in the comments or reach out directly.

Cheers Martin

entry.title: First smoke test after SAP LogServ solution deployment in Microsoft Sentinel - Part 0
entry.content:
Find your way to our central blog series entry here.

Dear community,

Due to the asynchronous integration between SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel, it's advisable to perform a smoke test yourself before sharing your config data with SAP. This helps confirm if your receiving connector setup works and ensures the correct configuration will be shared with SAP the first time round.

 

Run a smoke test for ease of mind

Once the SAP LogServ push-connector is deployed on Microsoft Sentinel, you can start posting data towards its data collection endpoint. Use the provided REST client collection, or scripts to replicate the exact requests details that LogServ is performing too.

Maintain the variables as per your environment, see the LogServ sample payload and start posting. A working request shows http code 204 with no content on the response. See a sample output from the provided powershell script for reference. I prefer REST clients but people are different 😎

 

Find the parameters from the SAP LogServ Sentinel connector wizard on initial connect (see screenshot below). You can also get them directly from the individual resources. The data collection endpoint and data collection rule are created in the same resource group as your Log Analytics workspace underpinning Sentinel. They are prefixed with “SAPLogServ”. The app registration on Entra ID can be found from the Entra Application ID field.

 

Use the 2 KQL queries below to inspect your first data entry on the SAPLogServ_CL table in Sentinel.

SAPLogServ_CL
| take 10
SAPLogServ_CL
| summarize count() by clz_dir, clz_subdir
| order by clz_dir asc, clz_subdir asc

 

Wait a minute or two on first try. The population of new tables takes a short time initially.

 

Hurray you have a functioning data collection endpoint for the SAP LogServ data🎉

Now, continue your onboarding journey by sharing the config data with SAP over your chosen channel. Find the details on SAP's blog here.

What if you don’t see the data entry on the LogServ table?

  1. Verify the connection details: Did you really hit the right endpoint? Are you looking in the right tenant and log analytics workspace?
  2. Verify selection criteria: Is your Time range filter accurate? The smoke tests create entries for NOW or 30mins earlier in UTC.
  3. Did enough time pass on the initial table population? Consider a retry of the KQL query.
  4. Verify the Data collection rule has not been changed to filter all results or similar. See this blog part and KQL snippet for more details.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap🌯. You saw today how to streamline your onboarding experience of SAP LogServ in Microsoft Sentinel. Simple smoke tests ensure a speedy onboarding and confidence in the shared setup configuration.

#Kudos to @Hemanth_Kusampudi and team for the partnership.

Get started with your deployment from here today.

Cheers Martin

entry.title: AI Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FSP03
entry.content:
In this blog post, let's delve into the innovations delivered with Feature Pack Stack 03 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, which was released in March 2025. We will focus on the cutting-edge innovations powered by artificial intelligence, revolutionizing how businesses operate and thrive. For an in-depth overview of the release, check out the latest update from our expert @Harish_Mangtani and his blog on this Cloud ERP solution.

The recent release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition introduces over 300 innovations, further enhancing capabilities across diverse business lines and industries. Key highlights include:

  • 200+ Innovations for Lines of Business
  • 60+ Innovations for Industry-Specific Needs
  • Numerous Localization and Cross-Topic Improvements

 

Join us as we explore how these innovations, especially in artificial intelligence, are setting new benchmarks in digital transformation, enabling businesses to harness data-driven insights for optimized decision-making and strategic growth.

Don’t miss this quick video that highlights some key innovations showcased in this post.

Please note that the following video was produced with Synthesia, a new platform that allows users to create videos featuring a digital twin of yourself and even includes a personal AI-generated Voice for an authentic touch.

  • Faster access to updates – Videos are created more frequently, so you're always getting the latest and most accurate information
  • Engaging and accessible – Short, to-the-point videos
  • More inclusive – Videos include subtitles and can be consumed in multiple languages

 

Watch now and let us know what you think in the comments!

Product Highlights

As enterprises look towards the future, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03 leads the charge with seven groundbreaking AI innovations. These advancements are designed to streamline operations, enhance financial accuracy, and optimize resource management, setting the stage for effective and sustainable business transformation.

1. AI-Assisted Journal Uploads

Finance departments can now leverage generative AI for journal uploads, automating the creation and validation processes. By assigning guidance documents and automatically generating posting proposals, businesses ensure accuracy and compliance. This allows financial professionals to pivot their focus towards strategic financial planning, supported by a reliable audit trail. 

 

 

 

2. AI-Assisted In-House Service Initiation
Repair processing becomes seamless with AI extracting vital data from scanned documents. The system reads these documents, proposes necessary repairs, and reduces manual efforts significantly. Automated identification of equipment further enhances efficiency, offering a higher degree of accuracy and reducing the likelihood of errors in repair processing.

 


3. Joule-Enabled Mass Modification of Sales Orders

Sales Order Management sees significant enhancement using Joule's Copilot capabilities. Performing mass changes to sales orders is now faster and more efficient, as users can update multiple orders simultaneously by applying a simple command. This innovation minimizes the need for manual updates and enhances overall efficiency in sales order management.

 


4. AI-Based Explanation Assistant for Detailed Scheduling

For production planning, the AI-based Explanation Assistant offers clear insights into scheduling results. It provides real-time feedback and detailed summaries, enabling production planners to understand and trust the outcomes of the Detailed Scheduling Optimizer. This enhances transparency and ensures smooth operations, driving efficiency in production environments.

 

5. Project System Support with Joule Copilot Capabilities

Project management is revolutionized with Joule's new project assistant capabilities. Project system users can now access instant data and status updates using everyday language queries, eliminating the need for complex navigation in multiple systems. This innovation streamlines data consolidation, providing a comprehensive overview of a project’s progress with ease.

 

6. Joule-Enhanced Search for Bills of Materials

Selective Joule scenarios enhance exploration capabilities within Bills of Materials. Users can utilize simple, natural language queries to search product data efficiently. This is particularly beneficial for R&D engineers and change coordinators, enabling cost-effective product lifecycle management and increased flexibility in change-record operations.

 

7. Intelligent Vehicle Assignment in Automotive Management

In automotive vehicle management, intelligent assignment capabilities ensure optimal matching between vehicle configurations and sales orders. The intuitive app streamlines inventory management by optimizing vehicle assignments and facilitates swaps or unassignments efficiently, improving the overall processing speed.

 

For more details on the innovations mentioned, watch the video and use the chapter markers to skip directly to the topics you're most interested in.

Summary and what to do next

As you can see, we have a lot to offer, very much based on feedback of clients running their business on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

Thanks for your interest in this product update for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts across the Cloud ERP portfolio.

SAP S/4HANA  

 Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:
